title: Examen oral — Preparación
goals: Preparate para el Speaking Test de Inglés I: exposición de un texto, datos personales y profundización.
Paso 1: exponé uno de los textos de matemática de la Unidad 5. Pasos 2 y 3: preguntas con respuesta modelo y variantes para practicar con Ping-Pong.

aspects.title: Paso 1 — Exponé un texto (1–1½ min)
aspects.summary: Preparás y grabás tu exposición oral sobre uno de los textos de matemática de la Unidad 5: título, tema, ideas principales, un ejemplo y la conclusión.

aspects.title: Paso 2 — Datos personales
aspects.summary: Las preguntas de datos personales del examen, con respuestas y variantes.

aspects.title: Paso 3 — Profundización
aspects.summary: Las preguntas de profundización: familia, barrio, rutinas, frecuencia y más.

aspects.title: Frases útiles del examen
aspects.summary: Pedí que repitan, ganá tiempo, decí que no sabés una palabra, saludá y cerrá con naturalidad.

source.note: Frase de apoyo para abrir la exposicion de un texto.

atoms.text: Today I'm going to talk about a text called 'What is a square?'.
atoms.gloss: Hoy voy a hablar de un texto llamado '¿Qué es un cuadrado?'.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: tədˈeɪ aɪm ɡˌoʊɪŋ tə tˈɔːk ɐbˌaʊt ɐ tˈɛkst kˈɔːld wʌt ɪz ɐ skwˈɛɹ

source.note: Frase de apoyo para explicar el tema del texto.

atoms.text: The text explains what a square is and what happens on curved surfaces.
atoms.gloss: El texto explica qué es un cuadrado y qué pasa en superficies curvas.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə tˈɛkst ɛksplˈeɪnz wʌt ɐ skwˈɛɹ ɪz ænd wʌt hˈæpənz ˌɔn kˈɜːvd sˈɜːfɪsᵻz

source.note: Frase de apoyo para cerrar la exposicion.

atoms.text: In conclusion, that's the main idea of the text. Thank you.
atoms.gloss: En conclusión, esa es la idea principal del texto. Gracias.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɪn kəŋklˈuːʒən ðæts ðə mˈeɪn aɪdˈiə ʌvðə tˈɛkst θˈæŋk juː

source.note: Exposicion oral del Paso 1 del Speaking Test (1-1.5 min): presentar uno de los textos de la Unidad 5. El modelo usa 'What is a square?'.

atoms.prompt: Present one of the maths texts from Unit 5 for one to one-and-a-half minutes, as in the oral exam (Step 1): title, topic, main ideas, an example and a conclusion.
atoms.modelAnswer: Today I'm going to talk about a text called 'What is a square?', by Hannah Darken. The text is about the definition of a square and how it changes on curved surfaces. First, it explains that on a flat surface, a shape with four equal sides and four right angles is a square. Then it introduces curvature: a plane is flat, a sphere curves outward, and a saddle has negative curvature. For example, on a sphere you can draw a triangle with three right angles that add up to two hundred and seventy degrees. On a pseudosphere, you can even draw shapes with equal sides and right angles that have more than four sides. In conclusion, equal sides and right angles do not always make a square: it all depends on the curvature of the space. That's my presentation. Thank you.
modelVariants: Today I'm going to talk about a text called 'Should people learn maths until age 18?'. The text argues that everyone should study maths until eighteen. First, it says maths opens doors to many careers, because it is the language of science and technology. Then it explains that maths is useful for everyday life: it helps us make informed decisions and understand statistics. The text also says maths teaches transferable skills, like problem-solving and healthy skepticism. In conclusion, learning maths until eighteen widens opportunities and develops skills for life. Thank you.

Today I'm going to talk about an interview called 'Why 2 Is the Best Number', from Scientific American. It is about a mathematician, Melanie Wood, who works in number theory. She explains that her job is to invent new methods, not just to apply them. Her tools are looking at many examples, finding patterns, and learning from her mistakes. Her favourite number is two, because it is simple but powerful: it creates the idea of even and odd numbers. In conclusion, the text shows that mathematics also needs creativity. Thank you.
scaffold: Decí el título del texto y de qué trata.
Explicá los puntos o secciones clave, en orden.
Cerrá con la conclusión o tu opinión, y agradecé.

steps.prompt: What is the title of the text and what is it about?
steps.segment: Today I'm going to talk about a text called 'What is a square?'. It is about the definition of a square and how it changes on curved surfaces.
On a flat surface, a shape with four equal sides and four right angles is a square.
The text explains curvature: a plane is flat, a sphere curves outward, and a saddle has negative curvature.

steps.prompt: Can you give an example from the text?
steps.segment: For example, on a sphere you can draw a triangle with three right angles that add up to two hundred and seventy degrees.

steps.prompt: What is the conclusion or your opinion?
steps.segment: In conclusion, equal sides and right angles do not always make a square: it depends on the curvature of the space.

rubric.text: Explicás los puntos principales en orden

atoms.speaker: student

source.note: Pregunta del Speaking Test oficial (Level 1). Respuestas y variantes nuestras.

atoms.prompt: What's your surname?

It's Garcia, G-A-R-C-I-A.
Garcia. It's a Spanish surname.
My family name is Martinez.
It's Rossi, an Italian surname.
atoms.speaker: examiner

source.note: Pregunta del Speaking Test oficial (Level 1). Respuestas y variantes nuestras.

atoms.prompt: Can you spell it, please?
replies: Yes, of course: G-A-R-C-I-A.
Sure, it's G-A-R-C-I-A.
G, A, R, C, I, A.
It's M-A-R-T-I-N-E-Z.
Of course. R, O, S, S, I. Rossi.
atoms.speaker: examiner

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: Good morning. Nice to meet you.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɡˈʊd mˈɔːɹnɪŋ nˈaɪs tə mˈiːt juː
alternatives: Good afternoon. Pleased to meet you.
Hello. It's a pleasure to meet you.

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.speaker: narrator
alternatives: I'm ready to start.
Yes, I'm ready, thank you.

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: Sorry, could you repeat that, please?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: sˈɑːɹi kʊd juː ɹᵻpˈiːt ðˈæt plˈiːz
alternatives: Could you say that again, please?
Pardon? Could you repeat the question?

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: Could you speak more slowly, please?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: kʊd juː spˈiːk mˈoːɹ slˈoʊli plˈiːz
alternatives: A little slower, please.
Could you slow down a bit, please?

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: Sorry, I don't understand the question.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: sˈɑːɹi aɪ dˈoʊnt ˌʌndɚstˈænd ðə kwˈɛstʃən
alternatives: What do you mean, exactly?
Could you explain the question, please?

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: Let me think for a moment.
atoms.speaker: narrator
alternatives: Let me see…
Just a second, let me think.

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.speaker: narrator
alternatives: That's a good question.
Mmm, good question.

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.text: I'm not sure how to say it in English.
atoms.gloss: No estoy seguro/a de cómo decirlo en inglés.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪm nˌɑːt ʃˈʊɹ hˌaʊ tə sˈeɪ ɪɾ ɪn ˈɪŋɡlɪʃ
alternatives: I don't know the word in English, sorry.
How do you say this in English?

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.speaker: narrator
alternatives: Thanks a lot.
Thank you.

source.note: Frase de interacción para el examen oral.

atoms.speaker: narrator
alternatives: That's everything, thank you.
I think that's all, thank you.

source.note: Conector genérico para exponer un texto (Paso 1).

atoms.text: First, I'll tell you what the text is about.
atoms.gloss: Primero, les voy a contar de qué trata el texto.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: fˈɜːst aɪl tˈɛl juː wʌt ðə tˈɛkst ɪz ɐbˈaʊt
alternatives: Let me start with the main idea.
I'll begin with what the text is about.

source.note: Conector genérico para exponer un texto (Paso 1).

atoms.text: In my opinion, this text is very interesting.
atoms.gloss: En mi opinión, este texto es muy interesante.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɪn maɪ əpˈɪniən ðɪs tˈɛkst ɪz vˈɛɹi ˈɪntɹɛstɪŋ
alternatives: I find this text interesting.
I think this topic is important.

source.note: Conector genérico para exponer un texto (Paso 1).

atoms.text: To sum up, that is the main idea.
atoms.gloss: En resumen, esa es la idea principal.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: tə sˈʌm ˈʌp ðæt ɪz ðə mˈeɪn aɪdˈiə
alternatives: In short, that's the key point.
So, that's the main idea of the text.
